architecture.summary: A two-stage fine-tuning approach for LLMs in Text-to-SQL tasks. Stage 1 (upstream): pre-trains the model on domain-specific database knowledge through three learning objectives—semantic enhancement of column names via cell values, semantic enhancement of table names, and schema knowledge injection to boost table-column name co-occurrence frequency. Stage 2 (downstream): standard fine-tuning on Text-to-SQL task data. Knowledge is injected as generated synthetic training data constructed from database schemas and cell values using question-answer templates, formatted as text generation tasks. Information flows from database (schema + sampled cell values) → template-filled synthetic tasks → upstream training → frozen model weights → downstream fine-tuning → SQL generation.
trained_components: LLM base model parameters (deepseek-coder-6.7b, CodeLlama-7b/13b, WizardCoder-15B, Baichuan2-7B) during both upstream knowledge injection and downstream Text-to-SQL fine-tuning

biblio.title: Enhancing Text-to-SQL Capabilities of Large Language Models via Domain Database Knowledge Injection

claims.statement: deepseek-coder-6.7b shows EX metric improvement of 3.1% and 77.8% EX baseline increasing to 80.9% after knowledge injection
CodeLlama-13b-Instruct shows EM metric improvement of 3.4% from 71.1% to 73.5% with knowledge enhancement
Baichuan2-7B-Chat general model shows EX improvement of 2.8% and EM improvement of 2.5% with knowledge injection

claims.metric: Column name accuracy (ratio of correct generated column names to total)
claims.statement: Column name generation accuracy improved by 2% to 4% across models after knowledge injection; deepseek-coder-6.7b accuracy increased from 0.73 to 0.77

Knowledge-injected models show improved robustness on Spider-Syn dataset (questions with synonym-substituted column/table names), with both EX and EM metrics higher than baseline, indicating more effective semantic understanding of column and table names

Performance on unseen databases (Spider test set) remained similar to baseline, indicating knowledge injection enhances understanding of learned domain-specific databases without degrading generalization to unknown databases

Mixing domain knowledge injection data with Text-to-SQL training data and training together caused performance degradation; CodeLlama-7b EX dropped from 76.6% to 73.8%, attributed to misalignment between upstream and downstream training objectives and data imbalance

Method has limited effectiveness in improving instruction comprehension and complex SQL query generation quality; even with knowledge injection, model comprehension of complex instructions remains unsatisfactory
